title: Hirpora Wildlife Sanctuary
tagline: Hirpora Wildlife Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Jammu & Kashmir

overview.short: Hirpora Wildlife Sanctuary or Heerpora Wildlife Sanctuary is located in Kulgam district and Shopian district of Jammu and Kashmir.
overview.description: Hirpora Wildlife Sanctuary or Heerpora Wildlife Sanctuary is located in Kulgam district and Shopian district of Jammu and Kashmir. It lies 12 km from Shopian town, 70 km (43 mi) south of Srinagar. It spreads over an area of 341 km2 (132 sq mi). It is bounded to the north by Lake Gumsar, northeast by Hirpora village, east by Rupri, south by Saransar and to the west by the Pir Panjal pass.

topPlaces.name: Battle of Hastivanj
topPlaces.description: The Battle of Hastivanj was fought between armies of Mughal Empire under Qasim Khan and Kashmir Sultanate under Shams Chak. It was fought on 10 October 1586 on a mountain ridge known as Hastivanj near modern-day Pir Ki Gali Pass.

topPlaces.name: Nandan Sar Lake
topPlaces.description: The Nandan Sar Lake is an oval shaped alpine lake located, near Poonch town in the Pir Panjal Range in the Poonch district of Jammu and Kashmir, India.

topPlaces.name: Pir Panjal Pass
topPlaces.description: The Pir Panjal Pass, also called Peer Ki Gali (or Peer Gali), is a mountain pass and a tourist destination located in the Pir Panjal Range in Jammu and Kashmir, India. It connects the Kashmir Valley to the Poonch district of Jammu & Kashmir via the Mughal Road.

items.note: The Battle of Hastivanj was fought between armies of Mughal Empire under Qasim Khan and Kashmir Sultanate under Shams Chak. It was fought on 10 October 1586 on a mountain ridge known as Hastivanj near modern-day Pir Ki Gali Pass.
The Nandan Sar Lake is an oval shaped alpine lake located, near Poonch town in the Pir Panjal Range in the Poonch district of Jammu and Kashmir, India.

The Pir Panjal Pass, also called Peer Ki Gali (or Peer Gali), is a mountain pass and a tourist destination located in the Pir Panjal Range in Jammu and Kashmir, India. It connects the Kashmir Valley to the Poonch district of Jammu & Kashmir via the Mughal Road.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Hirpora Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Hirpora Wildlife Sanctuary is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Hirpora Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Sheikh ul-Alam International Airport, Srinagar (46 km) and the nearest railway station is Katra (Vaishno Devi railhead) (75 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Hirpora Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Hirpora Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Hirpora Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Hirpora Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Hirpora Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Battle of Hastivanj, Nandan Sar Lake, Pir Panjal Pass.
